raw.title: How to build community resilience in the face of abrupt economic decline
raw.excerpt: Benicia, California, is set to lose its largest single employer and source of tax revenue, a sprawling oil refinery. A Guardian article about this issue catalyzed this essay that describes what a thoughtful process might look like, if a community - any community - chose to pursue sustainability instead of economic growth.

llm_processed.summary: The article suggests strategies for building community resilience in response to economic decline, specifically referencing a town facing challenges due to an oil refinery. It advocates for fostering social cohesion, diversified livelihoods, and prioritizing community well-being over constant economic growth.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: This case study provides practical insights into how communities can navigate economic transitions and build resilience through mutual support and alternative economic models.
